# Coding Plan for openclaw/openclaw#83134: Auto-generated VAPID keys use @localhost subject, breaking Apple Web Push (iOS PWA)

Source report: [records/openclaw-openclaw/items/83134.md](records/openclaw-openclaw/items/83134.md)

## Summary

Keep open: current main and v2026.5.12 still default Web Push VAPID to `mailto:openclaw@localhost`, and the send path passes that subject to `web-push`. The upstream `web-push` README documents `https:`/`mailto:` VAPID subjects and specifically notes Safari `BadJwtToken` failures for localhost subjects, so the reported iOS PWA failure is source-backed and narrow.

## Plan

Fix the Web Push VAPID default subject for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83134. Keep the change narrow: replace `DEFAULT_VAPID_SUBJECT = "mailto:openclaw@localhost"` with a stable valid OpenClaw-owned `https:` or `mailto:` URI, preserve `OPENCLAW_VAPID_SUBJECT` precedence for existing operators, and do not add an `openclaw.json` config surface unless maintainers explicitly request it. Update `src/infra/push-web.test.ts` assertions and `docs/web/control-ui.md` to document the corrected default. Add a concise changelog Fixes entry if the landing lane requires one for this user-visible release fix. Do not delete existing VAPID keypairs or change subscription storage behavior.

## Likely Files

- `src/infra/push-web.ts`
- `src/infra/push-web.test.ts`
- `docs/web/control-ui.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
